title: fix(lock): Fix LockError message when releasing a lock.
body: This change aligns the error messaging for releasing a lock that isn't owned with the expected behavior from tests (e.g., #3535). Previously, attempting to release an unlocked lock raised a LockError with the message "Cannot release an unlocked lock". The test expectation is that a release of a non-owned/locked state should surface the ownership-related error message: "Cannot release a lock that's no longer owned". To reflect ownership issues consistently, release() now raises LockNotOwnedError with that message when there is no local token, matching other ownership-related failures in the locking logic.

step_plan.summary: This change aligns the Lock.release error messaging for releases of non-owned/unlocked locks to reflect ownership semantics, matching tests and other failure paths.

steps.operation: Modify release() to raise LockNotOwnedError with ownership message when there is no local token
steps.reason: To ensure releasing a lock that isn't owned surfaces an ownership-related error, aligning with tests (e.g., #3535) and other ownership failure paths like do_release
steps.side_effects: Tests may be updated to expect LockNotOwnedError instead of LockError for this path; downstream code catching LockError on release may need adjustment; ensures API consistency for ownership-related failures; normal release flow using a valid token remains unchanged
